name: Robert Foster
subject: Franklin Graham
body:
Momentum is building
---
| | | | | | Friend,
It was encouraging to Heather and me as we awoke to Rev. Franklin Graham sharing our story on Facebook this morning.
It is humbling to see an outpouring of support from across the country since we were attacked for standing our ground on the principles so many of us share.
That is why I wanted to take a moment and introduce you to some of those who have donated in the last week:

I wish more candidates and politicians in Minnesota would follow your example! Thank you for having courage & standing firm for your honorable convictions. - Betty M (Minnesota)
Stand strong brother. Mississippi needs a man with your values, We are pulling for you in Tallahatchie county! - Kyle M (Mississippi)
I am not a constituent! But I watched your interview on CNN and have nothing but respect for you and for the vows you keep with your wife! You were clear and concise, no in-between. I will do my part with a donation to your campaign. - Terry B. (Hawaii)

There's no doubt our momentum is stronger than ever. But with 15 days left, we can't stop now.
[Please consider making a small donation here to keep our momentum going. ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001_bQFIJn72itsaEjywIyXTKizBkWVIWDkFMqh3AtnIph11-nP9v8fWc92Lv_W1gxUnGjbMTN6VEsAEsYiJ4DxOwHlZTmNUo3KLmzZVAx9S1ZU7sR7YCLSl_7TfS7DGM5G_bxYyxMsKVBqcddYeOk6I86ewy_MLld8mxyDIR6Cwe6Cm0wYbw5JvTPJWfpHjbScRHbmsAJTlifog6-_CA28MsIDmXu1Na6m&c=sQCnwc9gc4GE_EXE-bQNJN_TH7jWWFkEN4ogzFjF6YSSnXYAaqgpVA==&ch=AoxAe4iIVhVIytrnbhLUg86udM5oxHFSwXBNAbwy-DNV7x8Ro4inhQ==)
Every dollar helps, and we couldn't have gotten this far without people like yourself.
Gratefully,
Robert

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: Defending American Values
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501139615%2501July%2bNewsletter%250115408.13703140.4292916.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501139615%2501July%2bNewsletter%250115408.13703140.4292916.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **July 22, 2019** |
---|---|---|---
| | |
### True Patriots Must Defend the Values that Make America Great
President Trump's racist remarks telling four congresswomen of color to "go back" to where they came from because they don't like his policies represents a new low in the bottomless cesspool of his presidency. It is the latest reminder of why true American patriots from across the political spectrum must rally to defend the principles and values of our great country. We know that to love America is to strive everyday to build a more perfect union that achieves the goal of liberty and justice for all.
### Mitch McConnell Blocking Vital Measures Passed by the House
Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell continues to dig an ever deeper legislative graveyard by refusing to bring up a bevy of vital House-passed legislation supported by overwhelming majorities of the American people. The House-passed measures would: strengthen gun safety with universal background checks; reduce barriers to voting and end secret money in elections; reduce prescription drug costs; protect the Affordable Care Act; address climate change; provide equal pay for equal work; end workplace discrimination based on sexual orientation; protect Dreamers and TPS recipients; and phase-in an increase in the federal minimum wage to $15 an hour. While Senator McConnell and his GOP cohorts are blocking these popular measures, President Trump is seeking to dismantle big parts of our healthcare system through Executive Orders and by asking the courts to strike down the Affordable Care Act. If they are successful, over 130 million Americans will lose access to affordable care and tens of millions with pre-existing health conditions will lose the protections they now have. We must continue to enlist the public in our fight to demand that the Senate vote on the House-passed bills and end the Trump Administration's attack on key parts of our healthcare system.
### Bipartisan Senate Action on National Security Issues
The one recent exception to Senator McConnell's blocking of important measures was the passage of legislation to address some key national security matters. Specifically, the Senate passed the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), which contains funding for a pay increase for our men and women in uniform and provides them with needed support and equipment.

Over the Fourth of July, I was able to share this important news with some of our troops because I had the honor of spending this year's Independence Day with the men and women serving aboard the U.S.S. Abraham Lincoln in the Sea of Oman near the Persian Gulf. After landing on the carrier, I had the chance to speak to a number of F-18 pilots and others from Maryland. We discussed their day-to-day activities, and I told them our state was proud and grateful for their service.

In addition to providing a pay raise for our troops, the NDAA contains funding to support over five military installations in Maryland that perform important missions for our country. I'm also pleased that this bill incorporated the bipartisan legislation I authored with Senator Pat Toomey (R-Pa.) that closes the loopholes in the sanctions regime against North Korea. Our bill gives foreign banks a stark choice: continue doing business with North Korea or maintain access to the U.S. financial system. I believe this additional economic pressure on North Korea is necessary to achieve our goal of negotiating the denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula. Additionally, in order to protect the integrity of NATO's capabilities, I co-authored the measure that would prevent Turkey from obtaining the advanced F-35 jet if it takes delivery of the Russian S-400 air defense system. Since the NDAA passed, Turkey made a very bad decision and accepted delivery of the Russian system. Consequently, as called for in our legislation (and a similar measure I sponsored last year), the U.S. will not provide it with the F-35s, and the Trump Administration must impose certain sanctions on Turkey.

While the NDAA contains many important provisions, it is far from perfect. The overall level of funding authorized is excessive and some of the weapon systems it calls for are unnecessary or, worse in the case of certain low-yield nuclear systems, destabilizing. I will work to fix these problems as the bill winds through the legislative process. I also strongly support a provision in the House version of the NDAA that makes it clear that the President cannot go to war with Iran without congressional authorization. I will fight to ensure that the final bill includes that provision.
### Around the State/Baltimore County
Lastly, whenever possible, I try to visit different parts of our great state. Recently I spent a day visiting with folks in Baltimore County. We started the day in Randallstown, where I held a community meeting alongside Councilman Julian Jones, Speaker Adrienne Jones, and Delegate Ben Brooks. It was great to hear the thoughts, ideas, and concerns of all who attended. One issue that came up repeatedly was the need to invest more in a quality education for every child, no matter their zip code. That's why I've introduced the Keep Our Promises to America's Children and Teachers (Keep Our PACT) Act, which will finally mandate the expenditure of the full amount of federal public education money that has been promised--but not delivered--for Title I schools (those in lower-income areas) and IDEA (for special education). Following our meeting in Randallstown, I sat down with small business leaders at the Towson Chamber of Commerce. Ensuring our economic success means supporting small business growth, and I'll continue to support their priorities in the Congress. Our next stop was at Moon Valley Farm--one of the few woman-owned farms in Baltimore County. In addition to growing organic produce, they help train younger, start-up farmers, a goal I have supported in the Congress. In fact, I authored legislation that was included in the 2018 Farm Bill to reauthorize a critical program that helps these new farmers get the training and support they need to succeed. Our final stop of the day was at the new Guinness Brewery in Halethorpe. Maryland is proud that Guinness chose our state as the home for its first U.S.-based brewery opened since 1954. Since its opening, the Brewery has brought jobs, increased tourism, and added economic revenue to the area. Cheers!

Thank you for staying engaged in the debates that are vital to the health and future of our democracy! Chris Van Hollen
---

subject: Vice President Mike Pence: We’re Heading Back to the Moon and Then on to Mars
body:
"Unity is the true legacy of Apollo 11 – and we must capture that same unity in our own day by renewing our commitment to..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0055608dd1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Vice President Mike Pence: We’re Heading Back to the Moon and Then on to Mars**

Saturday marked “the 50th anniversary of the Apollo 11 moon landing, when ‘one small step for man’ became ‘one giant leap for mankind,’” Vice President Mike Pence writes in Fox News. “Unity is the true legacy of Apollo 11 – and we must capture that same unity in our own day by renewing our commitment to American leadership in space.”

Under President Donald J. Trump’s order, “the first woman and the next man on the moon will both be American astronauts, launched by American rockets, from American soil. The United States will lead the creation of a base at the moon’s South Pole where astronauts could reside for weeks and months,” the Vice President says.

“And from what we learn there, we will become the first nation in the history of mankind to set foot on the red planet of Mars.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6f9945f8a2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “To celebrate one year since President Donald Trump's ‘Pledge to America's Workers’ was announced, adviser Ivanka Trump and U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services Alex Azar visited Hy-Vee's innovation center in Grimes — and shot some hoops while they talked job training,” Robin Opsahl reports for the [_Des Moines Register_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ea68de7783&e=3ee4985eea). “Ivanka Trump said Hy-Vee — and Iowa as a whole — are great examples of workforces with high participation and room for advancement.”
---
|
---
| “If migrants are already safe in another country, why shouldn’t they apply for asylum there before coming to the United States and applying here? That’s the perfectly sound logic behind a rule Team Trump put in place Monday: Migrants who pass through another country will be ineligible for US asylum unless they’ve first sought it, unsuccessfully, in that third country,” the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7041612e30&e=3ee4985eea) editorial board writes. “Even with lax asylum rules, courts wind up denying 80 percent of the requests — eventually. Yet the backlog ensures that this can take years.”
---
|
---
| “Speaking at Kennedy Space Center on the 50th anniversary of the Apollo 11 Moon landing, Vice President Mike Pence announced that the Orion capsule that will take American astronauts back to the Moon is ready,” James Rogers reports for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ca2563ee20&e=3ee4985eea). “The Artemis program will land American astronauts on the Moon by 2024 and establish a sustainable human presence on Earth’s natural satellite. Artemis will also make history by landing the first woman on the Moon.”
---
|
---
| “U.S. Department of Agriculture Secretary Sonny Perdue will visit San Diego County Monday to tour local businesses and participate in a signing ceremony for the Trump administration's Pledge to America's Workers Initiative,” Kristina Houck reports for [_Patch_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=316d49c695&e=3ee4985eea). “While there, a signing ceremony will be held for the Pledge to America's Workers, a federal agreement with private sector businesses to create education and job training opportunities for workers in an effort to boost private sector job growth.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a3bce9127e&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8075e78653&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=f3ea26dd82)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Sign your name: Our plan to head off an economic crisis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
The country's economic foundation is fragile. A single shock could bring it all down. And the Trump administration's reckless behavior is increasing the odds. The administration may hit the debt... | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e996/45c30650/14387b819/669e5041/594714123/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
I've spent most of my career studying what's happening to working families in America. And when I saw the seeds of the 2008 crisis growing, I rang the alarm as loud as I could.

Back then, people with the power to stop the crisis didn't listen. And when the crisis hit in 2008, working families lost it all while the big banks that broke the economy got a fat taxpayer bailout.

When I look at the economy today, I see a lot to worry about again. **The good news is that we have the chance to head off another crisis if we take bold action now to address the underlying problems in the economy.**

**[That 's why I've got a new plan to tackle these threats before it's too late -- by reducing household debt, limiting dangerous corporate behavior, strengthening manufacturing, and limiting potential risks to the economy. Read on to learn more, and add your name if you're ready to fight for this plan.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e996/45c30650/14387b819/669e5040/594714123/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDcyMmVtMS1mdWxsLWEifQ==/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-economic-crisis?source=20190722em1-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Here are some of the warning signs I see flashing in our economy:

A generation of stagnant wages and rising costs have forced American families to take on more debt than ever before. The student debt load has more than doubled since the financial crisis, and credit card debt is at another high. Families may be able to afford some of those debt payments now, but a slowdown in income could plunge families over a cliff.

Corporations are also deeply in debt. Leveraged lending -- lending to companies that are already seriously in debt -- has jumped by 40% since Trump took office. These bets look a lot like the pre-2008 subprime mortgages: poorly-underwritten high-risk loans, with minimal protections. Just like last time, their risk-taking is dangerous for all of us.

Despite Donald Trump's promises of a manufacturing "renaissance," the country is now in a manufacturing recession. The manufacturing sector has just gone through a second straight quarter of decline, and for the first time ever, the average hourly wage for manufacturing workers has dropped below the national average.

The country's economic foundation is fragile. A single shock could bring it all down. And the Trump administration's reckless behavior is increasing the odds. The administration may hit the debt ceiling in September, leading to economic turmoil that some have compared to the collapse of Lehman Brothers. Trump's trade war with China also threatens American manufacturing and has already hurt American companies that investors think of as bellwethers.

**But the good news is, we can still head off another crisis if we act now. Here 's my plan to put our economy -- and our families -- on firmer ground:

Reduce household debt.** We need to reduce household debt by raising wages and by bringing down working families' costs. **That is the heart of my economic agenda.**

We can raise incomes by increasing the minimum wage to $15 an hour, strengthening unions, and ensuring that women of color get the wages they deserve. We can slash household debt by cancelling student loan debt, bringing down the cost of rent, providing universal affordable child care, and making tuition free at public colleges.

**Monitor and reduce leveraged corporate lending:** In response to the 2008 crisis, Congress created the Financial Stability Oversight Council to monitor risks across that cut across different markets. But right now, Trump-era regulators are falling down on the job. They should focus on the risks posed by highly indebted companies and stop banks from issuing these risky loans in the first place.

**Strengthen manufacturing:** We need policies that reverse the manufacturing job losses of the past twenty years. My Green Manufacturing Plan will mobilize our industrial base by making a $2 trillion investment in American green research, manufacturing, and exporting over the next decade. This will create more than a million high-quality jobs and help address the existential threat of climate change.

**Limit potential shocks to the economy:** The Trump Administration should replace the trade-war-by-tweet with China with a coherent strategy -- working with our allies -- to respond to China's trade tactics. We need to invest in strengthening critical American industries, instead of undercutting American companies.

Whether it's this year or next year, the odds of another economic downturn are high -- and growing. Congress and regulators should act immediately to tamp down these threats.

**[Add your name and join this fight if you 're ready for our government to finally learn the lessons of 2008 -- and stop another crisis before it's too late.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e996/45c30650/14387b819/669e5040/594714123/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDcyMmVtMS1mdWxsLWEifQ==/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-economic-crisis?source=20190722em1-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being part of this,

Elizabeth

clean_body: The country's economic foundation is fragile. A single shock could bring it all down. And the Trump administration's reckless behavior is increasing the odds. The administration may hit the debt... | | | "" I've spent most of my career studying what's happening to working families in America. And when I saw the seeds of the 2008 crisis growing, I rang the alarm as loud as I could. Back then, people with the power to stop the crisis didn't listen. And when the crisis hit in 2008, working families lost it all while the big banks that broke the economy got a fat taxpayer bailout. When I look at the economy today, I see a lot to worry about again. The good news is that we have the chance to head off another crisis if we take bold action now to address the underlying problems in the economy. "" Here are some of the warning signs I see flashing in our economy: A generation of stagnant wages and rising costs have forced American families to take on more debt than ever before. The student debt load has more than doubled since the financial crisis, and credit card debt is at another high. Families may be able to afford some of those debt payments now, but a slowdown in income could plunge families over a cliff. Corporations are also deeply in debt. Leveraged lending -- lending to companies that are already seriously in debt -- has jumped by 40% since Trump took office. These bets look a lot like the pre-2008 subprime mortgages: poorly-underwritten high-risk loans, with minimal protections. Just like last time, their risk-taking is dangerous for all of us. Despite Donald Trump's promises of a manufacturing "renaissance," the country is now in a manufacturing recession. The manufacturing sector has just gone through a second straight quarter of decline, and for the first time ever, the average hourly wage for manufacturing workers has dropped below the national average. The country's economic foundation is fragile. A single shock could bring it all down. And the Trump administration's reckless behavior is increasing the odds. The administration may hit the debt ceiling in September, leading to economic turmoil that some have compared to the collapse of Lehman Brothers. Trump's trade war with China also threatens American manufacturing and has already hurt American companies that investors think of as bellwethers. But the good news is, we can still head off another crisis if we act now. Here 's my plan to put our economy -- and our families -- on firmer ground: Reduce household debt. We need to reduce household debt by raising wages and by bringing down working families' costs. That is the heart of my economic agenda. We can raise incomes by increasing the minimum wage to $15 an hour, strengthening unions, and ensuring that women of color get the wages they deserve. We can slash household debt by cancelling student loan debt, bringing down the cost of rent, providing universal affordable child care, and making tuition free at public colleges. Monitor and reduce leveraged corporate lending: In response to the 2008 crisis, Congress created the Financial Stability Oversight Council to monitor risks across that cut across different markets. But right now, Trump-era regulators are falling down on the job. They should focus on the risks posed by highly indebted companies and stop banks from issuing these risky loans in the first place. Strengthen manufacturing: We need policies that reverse the manufacturing job losses of the past twenty years. My Green Manufacturing Plan will mobilize our industrial base by making a $2 trillion investment in American green research, manufacturing, and exporting over the next decade. This will create more than a million high-quality jobs and help address the existential threat of climate change. Limit potential shocks to the economy: The Trump Administration should replace the trade-war-by-tweet with China with a coherent strategy -- working with our allies -- to respond to China's trade tactics. We need to invest in strengthening critical American industries, instead of undercutting American companies. Whether it's this year or next year, the odds of another economic downturn are high -- and growing. Congress and regulators should act immediately to tamp down these threats. "" Thanks for being part of this, Elizabeth | "" Elizabeth doesn't accept contributions from PACs of any kind or federally registered lobbyists. This grassroots movement is powered by supporters like you. subject: Reform our legislature (add your name)
body:

It's simple -- our government should answer to the voters, NOT special interests and out-of-state corporations.

Friend,

It's simple -- our government should answer to the voters, NOT special interests and out-of-state corporations. But for too long, Mississippi's legislative leadership has mismanaged taxpayer money and created policies that hurt working people in Mississippi. That's why Jim is running for governor -- to clean up corruption and create a government that's transparent and accountable. **Will you sign onto our petition showing your support for Jim 's legislative reforms?**
********[**ADD YOUR NAME**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-NlNM6e3Y_3BfBHd5ysRS0P3ELGOftAoQlTzx3iWymU=/ELth5cBhG7vLsii96CfRTlggixQ7amTb6j-PkO5Lv4k=)********
---
Jim Hood has a clear plan to take power and influence away from large special interests and put it back in the hands of the people of Mississippi. That means:
* Restricting corporate campaign contributions.

* Prohibiting campaign contributions while the legislature is in session.

* Subjecting our legislators to the Public Records Act like every other public official.

These reforms will increase transparency in our state government and make it so that our legislature is once again accountable to the people who vote for them.

**Sign our petition to support Jim 's proposal to clean up Mississippi's government.**

But we have the perfect plan to fight back: Get a record number of donations today to make Mike Pence regret EVER coming to Colorado -- and eliminate his Republican Senate Majority by electing an openly gay man. Quickly -- before Pence skips town, can you chip in $19 to elect Dan, flip the Senate, and stand up to Mike Pence? | | "" "" "" "" "" Dan isn't just the only openly gay man in this race -- he's also the most qualified candidate. He stood up to dictators and regimes around the world as an Obama Ambassador, and fought for Colorado's students in the Department of Higher Education. We need someone like him fighting for us in the Senate and taking on bigoted Republicans like Pence and Trump. "" Thanks, Team Baer "" "" Colorado is the most important Senate race in the entire country according to Nate Silver and CNN. It's our only chance of flipping the Senate. And to win the most important Senate race, we need the most qualified candidate. Dan served as an Ambassador under President Obama and as the head of the Colorado Department of Higher Education in Governor Hickenlooper's cabinet. If elected, he would be the first openly gay man to serve in Senate history. We know we send you a lot of emails, but it's because we're running a grassroots campaign and rejecting corporate PAC money. We need your help to win -- and grassroots donations like yours truly make the difference. | | "" If you'd like to receive fewer emails, please let us know "". We hope you'll consider sticking with us long-term. But if you'd like to unsubscribe, please "". | | Paid for by Dan for Colorado

name: Women for Wilmot
subject: We're off to an incredible start!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by A whole lot of folks for Wilmot Collins, PO Box 458, Helena, MT 59624
body:
#
Friend,
We only launched Women for Wilmot a week ago and we're already off to an incredible start!
Maddie Collins, Wilmot's wife and chair of Women for Wilmot, hosted our very first event in Helena where we engaged in a powerful discussion about what matters to Montana women.
[](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea50b/3754442653/VEsH/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/women-for-wilmot?source=em190722")
Maddie shared her story of when she first came to Helena as a young foreign exchange student, and of later meeting and marrying Wilmot and making their home in Montana. The group then talked about our shared values, our stories, campaign strategy, and more.
**[Want to be a part of future events like this one? Join Women for Wilmot now.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea508/3754442653/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/women-for-wilmot?source=em190722&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=")**
**[](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea50b/3754442653/VEsF/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/women-for-wilmot?source=em190722")**
In Billings, we launched Women for Wilmot with a passionate discussion about tackling the epidemic of missing and murdered indigenous women, ending the scourge of gun violence, and how we can all help elect Wilmot to the Senate block-by-block and neighbor-by-neighbor.
[](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea50b/3754442653/VEsC/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/women-for-wilmot?source=em190722")
And in Livingston, Women for Wilmot founding member Michelle Uberuaga Zanoni hosted a coffee and breakfast at her home where we shared laughs, strategy, and why we're fighting for our children and families on healthcare, climate change, pay equity, and public education.
**[Wilmot Collins will fight for Montana women and families in the Senate--but it's going to take all of us to make it happen. Become a founding member of Women for Wilmot now.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea508/3754442653/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/women-for-wilmot?source=em190722&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=")**
Women for Wilmot is also proud to announce our list of Founding Members. These women are trailblazers for Montana women and families and Wilmot is honored to have their support.
* Michelle Uberuaga Zanoni - Livingston
* Gerry Jennings - Great Falls
* Carolyn Pease Lopez - Billings
* Michele Shea - Butte
* Mariah Gladstone - Kalispell
* Kim Abbott - Helena
* Nancy de Pastino - Missoula
* Judith Heilman - Bozeman
* Christine Kauffmann - Helena
* Lauren Small Rodriguez - Missoula
* Rachel Carroll Rivas - Helena
* Liz Welch - Billings
* Jacquie McAdam - Butte
* Jess Wilkerson - Helena
* Pam Carroll - Great Falls
* Melissa Hornbein - Helena
* Romy McGahan Daniel - Missoula
* Pat Kemp - Helena

**[Working together, Montana women are going to elect someone who will fight for us in the Senate. Will you join us and become a founding member of Women for Wilmot now?](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea508/3754442653/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/women-for-wilmot?source=em190722&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=")**
Thanks for being a part of our team,
Women for Wilmot
**[JOIN WOMEN FOR WILMOT](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea508/3754442653/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/women-for-wilmot?source=em190722&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=")**

* * *
Paid for by A whole lot of folks for Wilmot Collins, PO Box 458, Helena, MT 59624
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Supporters like you are a crucial part of our team, but if you'd like to unsubscribe, you can [click here](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea509/3754442653/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). To change your email address or update your contact info, [click here.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea506/3754442653/VEsP/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/update-your-contact-info?source=footer") [Make a contribution now to elect Wilmot Collins as Montana's next U.S. Senator.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/561647ac/1e3760f0/69e3634d/5f7ea507/3754442653/VEsHBQ/?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=footer")

body:
Will you take my emergency Mueller Poll to help me ensure Mueller can testify before Congress? I need 10,000 responses before midnight. >>
---
| | **AG BARR: I will block Robert Mueller's team from testifying to Congress** | I NEED 10,000 MUELLER RESPONSES Quickly -- Robert Mueller is testifying before Congress in less than 48 hours -- _but Trump's attorney general just threatened to BLOCK the full truth from being revealed._ **I need a MASSIVE surge of responses before midnight to protect Mueller 's testimony. Do you want to hear what Mueller has to say?**

[**YES! >>**](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b50f/18e32a7f/575a2a11/7bb271b3/3091651493/VEsH/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/mueller-survey?source=20190722_em_pet_b1_mtvbrl_all_rs")** **[No](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b50f/18e32a7f/575a2a11/7bb271b3/3091651493/VEsE/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/mueller-survey?source=20190722_em_pet_b1_mtvbrl_all_rs")****
---

This is the most important ask I've ever made of you.

Robert Mueller will testify before Congress in less than 48 hours. We could finally get the TRUTH about his findings.

**But Donald Trump's attorney general just threatened to BLOCK the full truth from being revealed --_under no circumstances can we let him succeed. _**

I need a swift and MASSIVE surge of support before midnight to prove that Americans DEMAND to hear what Mueller has to say.

[_**Will you take my emergency Mueller Poll to help me ensure Mueller can testify before Congress WITHOUT interference from President Trump's administration? I need 10,000 responses before midnight. >> **_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b50f/18e32a7f/575a2a11/7bb271b3/3091651493/VEsF/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/mueller-survey?source=20190722_em_pet_b1_mtvbrl_all_rs")

Thank you,

Ben Ray Lujan

|

[](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b50f/18e32a7f/575a2a11/7bb271b0/3091651493/VEsC/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.facebook.com/BenRayLujan") [](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b50f/18e32a7f/575a2a11/7bb271b1/3091651493/VEsD/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://twitter.com/benraylujan") [](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b50f/18e32a7f/575a2a11/7bb271b6/3091651493/VEsA/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://www.instagram.com/benraylujan/?hl=en")
Ben Ray Lujan is running to be the first Hispanic Senator from New Mexico in more than 40 years. No one like Ben Ray has ever run for Senate before. He led the charge to take back the House for the American people and Democrats (and succeeded by a historic margin) and now he's running to take back the Senate too.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because Ben Ray isn't taking a cent of corporate PAC money. His campaign is 100% people-powered. And the polls show we have a once-in-a-lifetime shot to finally defeat Mitch McConnell and take back the Senate. But we can't do this without winning New Mexico. Will you be a part of this historic movement?
**[DONATE](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b50f/18e32a7f/575a2a11/7bb271b7/3091651493/VEsB/?g=YeDMcDpTVzRcsCgKwv9UzQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/brl_email?refcode=email_footer_donate&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")**

**This race is actually wide open. There are months to go before the primaries, and thousands of voters to meet and persuade.** [**_With your continued support, we can keep this a real contest._**](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190722_MES_20190722-fr-MES-countout-all_all_l1&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Here in Colorado, we know well that you can never predict what's going to happen in a race.

The unexpected happened when John ran for mayor of Denver. He began as a dark horse--a restaurateur and activist who'd revitalized downtown Denver but had never held office--and walked away the victor.

The unexpected happened both times John ran for Governor--including his re-election in a year that saw most Democrats swept out of office across Colorado.

As beloved Colorado journalist Lynn Bartels says: **Don 't count Hick out just yet.**

John is the only candidate who has run a small business, a city, and a state. And while doing it, he's gotten big, bold, progressive things done that others in this race have only talked about.

**As I said: You can never predict what 's going to happen in politics.** What we know is that, with your help, John is putting in the hard work to introduce himself to voters and share his vision in every community he travels to. That's the work that will truly pay off in this primary.

#
In just a moment, I am going to ask you to add a donation to our presidential campaign before next week’s debate. | In an important election year like 2019, we're out talking to Hoosiers every day, and what better place than the Indiana State Fair to tell Hoosiers about **Governor Eric Holcomb, Lieutenant Governor Suzanne Crouch, President Donald J. Trump,** and our fellow Hoosier and**Vice President, Mike Pence**?

And that's where you come in. We need your help!
* **_Be the Face of Your Indiana Republican Party_**
With the 17-day event, we need all-hands-on-deck to staff our booth and to talk with voters. [Sign-up here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=85ff95e847&e=1acd9bb3d6), and a member of our team will be in touch to schedule you for a three-hour shift (which includes a free ticket to the fair!).

* **_Stop by the Booth to Show Your Support_**
Plan to be at the fair, but don't have time to volunteer? Stop by our booth, which will be on the northwest side of the fairgrounds near the free stage.

This year, the Indiana State Fair runs from August 2-18. Make sure you don't miss out on the fun, and [either sign-up for a volunteer slot](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e4054e20b4&e=1acd9bb3d6) (**which includes a free ticket to the fair**) or make plans to stop by our booth to grab some swag, and sign petitions to get **President Trump** and **Governor Holcomb** on the ballot!

See you at the fair!

subject: Ivanka Trump on "an unbelievable story" in America’s heartland
body:
This past Friday marked one year since President Trump announced his Pledge to America’s Workers, a landmark effort... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=445a8cd57c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 22, 2019
---
| | |
# Ivanka Trump on "an unbelievable story" in America’s heartland
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=34b61d75a6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **This past Friday** marked one year since President Trump announced his Pledge to America’s Workers, a landmark effort to train and reskill our nation’s workforce.

**The results** have been incredible, with more than 9 million training opportunities pledged for American job seekers and students. In honor of the Pledge’s success, Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump traveled to Iowa last week to get a first-hand look at one of the training centers where American ingenuity is taking off.

**“Iowa** has an unbelievable story,” she said. “This is a great climate, a great opportunity. But it brings to bear a unique challenge, which is the fact that people need [skilled] workers.” More of those newly skilled workers will be prepared to step in soon: Hy-Vee CEO Randy Edeker committed to 15,000 more opportunities for U.S. workers on Friday.

**“This is exactly** why it's so important to leave D.C.,” Ms. Trump said.

[The Pledge to America’s Workers has nearly 10 million commitments in year one.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=919bfba81b&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **50 years later, it’s time for another ‘giant leap’ for mankind**

**On Saturday,** America celebrated the 50th anniversary of the first Moon landing.

**That achievement** captivated the world, including millions of Americans who watched history unfold before their eyes. “For one brief moment, all the people of the world were truly one,” Vice President Mike Pence said Saturday at the Kennedy Space Center.

** **** Watch:** [Vice President celebrates Moon landing with Apollo 11 astronauts](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=983626012c&e=3ee4985eea)

**“All at once,** the nation held its breath — as through the crackling broadcast we listened to, we heard Neil Armstrong use those immortal words: ‘That’s one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind,’” the Vice President said from Apollo 11’s launch site.

**“In that moment,** the men of Apollo 11 did more than help expand our understanding of Creation, and they did more than win the Space Race. They brought together our nation.”

**The Apollo 11** **mission** ignited enthusiasm across America for space exploration—a flame that unfortunately waned over time. It’s been more than 48 years since an American walked on the Moon. The National Space Council, disbanded in 1993, lay dormant for nearly a quarter of a century before President Donald J. Trump resurrected it.

**Americans are looking** to the stars once again. President Trump has committed to putting Americans back on the Moon by 2024. The United States will seek to establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon by 2028, and then chart a path forward for the exploration of Mars from there.

**“The****next man** —and the first woman—on the Moon will be American astronauts,” the Vice President said on Saturday. “We’re going back.”

[50 Years After Walking on the Moon, It’s Time for Another ‘Giant Leap’ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=51d5947109&e=3ee4985eea)

**Vice President Pence:** [We’re Heading Back to the Moon and Then on to Mars](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=648bc34f3a&e=3ee4985eea)

clean_body: This past Friday marked one year since President Trump announced his Pledge to America’s Workers, a landmark effort... | | | | | The White House • July 22, 2019 | | | Ivanka Trump on "an unbelievable story" in America’s heartland | | This past Friday marked one year since President Trump announced his Pledge to America’s Workers, a landmark effort to train and reskill our nation’s workforce. The results have been incredible, with more than 9 million training opportunities pledged for American job seekers and students. In honor of the Pledge’s success, Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump traveled to Iowa last week to get a first-hand look at one of the training centers where American ingenuity is taking off. “Iowa has an unbelievable story,” she said. “This is a great climate, a great opportunity. But it brings to bear a unique challenge, which is the fact that people need [skilled] workers.” More of those newly skilled workers will be prepared to step in soon: Hy-Vee CEO Randy Edeker committed to 15,000 more opportunities for U.S. workers on Friday. “This is exactly why it's so important to leave D.C.,” Ms. Trump said. | | 50 years later, it’s time for another ‘giant leap’ for mankind On Saturday, America celebrated the 50th anniversary of the first Moon landing. That achievement captivated the world, including millions of Americans who watched history unfold before their eyes. “For one brief moment, all the people of the world were truly one,” Vice President Mike Pence said Saturday at the Kennedy Space Center. Watch: “All at once, the nation held its breath — as through the crackling broadcast we listened to, we heard Neil Armstrong use those immortal words: ‘That’s one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind,’” the Vice President said from Apollo 11’s launch site. “In that moment, the men of Apollo 11 did more than help expand our understanding of Creation, and they did more than win the Space Race. They brought together our nation.” The Apollo 11 mission ignited enthusiasm across America for space exploration—a flame that unfortunately waned over time. It’s been more than 48 years since an American walked on the Moon. The National Space Council, disbanded in 1993, lay dormant for nearly a quarter of a century before President Donald J. Trump resurrected it. Americans are looking to the stars once again. President Trump has committed to putting Americans back on the Moon by 2024. The United States will seek to establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon by 2028, and then chart a path forward for the exploration of Mars from there. “Thenext man —and the first woman—on the Moon will be American astronauts,” the Vice President said on Saturday. “We’re going back.” Vice President Pence: | | Photo of the Day | Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead | President Donald J.
